name: Columbus Housing Partnership, INC, Dba Homeport
description_short: Columbus Housing Partnership (Homeport) is a nonprofit affordable housing developer and homebuyer educator serving Central Ohio with home development, financial fitness classes, and resident services.
description_long:
Columbus Housing Partnership, operating as Homeport, is a nonprofit organization based in Columbus, Ohio, dedicated to expanding affordable housing access and building economic stability for low- and moderate-income residents. Founded on a mission to create strong communities through quality, affordable homes built on dignity, security, and opportunity, Homeport operates across multiple service lines including residential development, homebuyer education, and ongoing resident support services.

Homeport's primary services include developing affordable for-sale homes and rental properties across Central Ohio, offering homebuyer education classes, providing financial fitness programming, delivering down payment assistance through programs like the Rise Up Fund, and providing targeted resident services to maintain housing stability. The organization manages multiple residential communities including Easton Place Homes, Fieldstone Cottages (senior living), Emerald Glen, and the recently announced Channing Street Homes development in Delaware County. They serve as a resource for affordable, quality housing and operate educational programming through Homeport Learning.

Homeport distinguishes itself as a comprehensive housing solutions provider rather than a traditional lender. The organization partners with community institutions (Park National Bank, State Auto, The Georgetown Company, LifeCare Alliance), maintains multilingual accessibility (English, Spanish, Somali, Pashto, Arabic, Farsi), and integrates resident services including senior dining programs and youth out-of-school programming. Their approach emphasizes dignity and community engagement alongside housing access, with documented impact in addressing Central Ohio's housing crisis.

As a nonprofit organization, Homeport is best suited for low- and moderate-income individuals and families seeking affordable housing with educational support rather than those needing traditional mortgage products. A significant caveat is that Homeport does not function as a direct mortgage lender—they develop and manage properties while offering educational and financial assistance services. Potential residents should contact them directly about current property availability and eligibility requirements.
pros: Offers free homebuyer education and financial fitness classes with multiple registration periods throughout the year
Provides down payment assistance through the Rise Up Fund program to qualifying buyers
Operates multiple affordable housing communities with both rental and for-sale options across Central Ohio
Delivers comprehensive resident services including senior dining programs, youth out-of-school programming, and community engagement activities
Maintains multilingual website accessibility in six languages (English, Spanish, Somali, Pashto, Arabic, Farsi)
Strong community partnerships with established financial institutions and nonprofits (Park National Bank, State Auto, LifeCare Alliance)
Actively expanding housing pipeline with recent developments like Channing Street Homes and Easton Phase 2 (100 energy-efficient homes)
cons: Does not directly issue mortgages—functions as a developer and educator rather than a lender, so those needing traditional financing should seek other sources
Limited transparency on website regarding specific property availability, pricing, and current occupancy status at their communities
No detailed information available about income eligibility requirements, down payment assistance amounts, or application processes
Organization requires direct contact for specific program details and property inquiries rather than providing comprehensive online information
Senior living communities (Fieldstone Cottages) may have specialized eligibility criteria not fully detailed on main website
best_for: Low- and moderate-income first-time homebuyers in Central Ohio seeking education and down payment assistance
Individuals and families looking for affordable rental housing in well-maintained communities with resident services
Senior citizens interested in affordable senior living communities with dining and wellness programming
Central Ohio residents seeking financial literacy education and homebuyer preparation before pursuing homeownership
diagnosis: Homeport is best for low- and moderate-income Central Ohio residents seeking affordable housing, homebuyer education, or financial literacy—not for those needing traditional mortgage financing. The main caveat is that while Homeport develops and manages affordable properties, they do not function as a mortgage lender and direct mortgage applicants should seek financing through traditional lenders or bank partners.
services: Homebuyer education classes (multiple sessions per year: December, January, February, March)
Financial fitness classes for credit and money management education
For-sale affordable home development and sales
Rental property development and leasing (multifamily communities)
Down payment assistance through the Rise Up Fund
Senior living community development and management
Resident services and community engagement programming
Youth out-of-school programming and educational initiatives
Senior dining center partnerships (through LifeCare Alliance collaboration)
Housing stability support and case management services
Community event programming (Voice & Vision annual conference)
Property management services at multiple residential communities
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
meta_description: Columbus Housing Partnership (Homeport) is a nonprofit affordable housing developer and homebuyer educator serving Central Ohio.
